exemplar.title: How does the magnetic flux density impact the magnitude of the braking force due to induced eddy currents for a track of constant dimensions and coefficient of static friction?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the magnetic flux density impact the magnitude of the braking force due to induced eddy currents for a track of constant dimensions and coefficient of static friction?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The title and research question are communicated precisely, establishing a clear investigative focus on how magnetic flux density affects eddy-current braking force.
Sectional headings, diagrams and repeated signposting maintain effective communication and reader orientation throughout.
The methodology is mostly complete with informed choices (e.g., repeated trials, calibration of B-field measurements) and appropriate controls of ancillary variables.
weaknesses: There is a slight loss of focus when the dependent variable is plotted as Fâ/v rather than the braking force Fâ itself.
The justification for uneven increments in magnetic flux density (e.g., 43â103â141 mT) is not provided, weakening the experimental design clarity.
The use of an indirect force determination (via F = m g sinÎ¸ â F_d) introduces conceptual compromise and could be improved by direct force measurement.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the investigation demonstrates a sustained and effective focus: the research question is clear and tightly connected to the theoretical and experimental work, and the topic is communicated with precision. Minor lapsesâsuch as the choice to plot Fâ/v instead of Fâ, uneven B-field increments without rationale, and an indirect force measurementâprevent a fully flawless methodology but do not obscure the studyâs purpose or validity. This performance resides firmly within the upper half of the 5â6 band, substantiating a score of 5.

strengths: The essay shows excellent command of electromagnetism: Faradayâs law, Lorentz force, Lenzâs law and skin-effect considerations are all accurately applied to derive Fâ â BÂ².
Technical language and symbols are defined and used consistentlyâterms like magnetic flux density, eddy current and terminal velocity are precise and coherent.
Free-body diagrams and mathematical expressions support conceptual clarity and demonstrate coherent understanding of the underlying physics.
weaknesses: Occasional simplifications (e.g., assuming perfectly perpendicular field lines and uniform flux) introduce minor inaccuracies.
Some theoretical citations (e.g., inverse-cube law from Griffiths) lack precise references, reducing academic rigor.
The macroscopic Lorentz expression Fâ = BIL uses an ambiguously defined L; a clearer definition of the eddy-current path length would strengthen subject-knowledge application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently applies relevant subject knowledge with coherent understanding and employs technical terminology accurately. While minor conceptual approximations and incomplete citations prevent an entirely flawless performance, the depth and accuracy of the physics analysis place it securely at the top of the 5â6 band. A score of 5 reflects this high level of knowledge and understanding.

strengths: The quantitative analysis is rigorous: multiple trials, uncertainty propagation, linear regression with RÂ² = 0.9908 and literature comparisons demonstrate strong research analysis.
Arguments progress logically from microscopic theory through macroscopic modeling to empirical verification, yielding a coherent and persuasive narrative.
The student identifies and proposes sensible methodological improvements (e.g., rotating brake rig, direct force measurement) that show depth of thought.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation remains largely qualitative; quantitative error estimates (e.g., skin-effect corrections, angle deviations) are absent.
Some reasoning stepsâsuch as the justification for using Fâ/v instead of Fââare asserted without full critical defence.
Alternative interpretations and methodological approaches are touched on briefly rather than explored in depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits excellent research analysis and well-developed argumentation, consistently focusing on the research question and supporting conclusions with strong empirical evidence. Critical evaluation, while good, is somewhat limited by qualitative treatment of errors and underdeveloped alternative perspectives. These qualities align with the upper range of the 7â9 band, justifying a score of 9 out of 12.

strengths: The essayâs structure follows IB conventions impeccably: title page with word count, comprehensive table of contents, numbered pages and well-labelled figures and tables.
Visual elementsâdiagrams, photographs and graphsâare generally effective and support clear communication of complex information.
Consistent formatting, font choice and spacing make navigation and comprehension straightforward.
weaknesses: Some figure captions are overly verbose and embedded in alt-text within the main body, which disrupts readability.
Unit usage is occasionally inconsistent between analysis (m sâ»Â¹) and figures (mm sâ»Â¹), risking confusion.
The bibliography mixes citation styles and lacks uniform formatting, detracting slightly from overall presentation polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all expectations for clear and appropriate structure with only minor lapses in caption concision, unit consistency and citation formatting. These issues do not impede comprehension or navigation, placing the work firmly in the top band (3â4). A maximum score of 4 is warranted.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical language is employed **consistently and with precision**: terms such as magnetic flux density, eddy current, Lorentz force, terminal velocity, skin effect and shape factor are used correctly.  Symbols are defined, units stated, and vector notation handled competently.  A few minor slips (e.g., occasional conflation of drag and rolling resistance; small formatting issues with symbols) prevent absolute perfection, but overall the terminology and conceptual deployment reflect a high level of mastery.  This fits securely within the top band; a score of 5 is appropriate.

The discussion weaves theory, method and findings into a **coherent narrative**: the student links the derived expression, experimental data and real-world applications (roller-coaster brakes).  Arguments progress logically from microscopic theory to macroscopic model, then to empirical verification.  Nonetheless, some reasoning steps (e.g., justification for plotting Fââv instead of Fâ) are asserted rather than critically defended, and alternative interpretations are only briefly touched upon.  The structure is clear and generally persuasive, meeting the descriptors for the 7-9 band, but the depth of argumentation is not consistently sophisticated; a score of 7 is awarded.

The student designs a workable apparatus using a motion cart, gaussmeter and 3-D-printed spacers to maintain constant separation, and identifies relevant variables with care (independent, dependent and controls). **Repeated trials, uncertainty treatment and calibration of B-field measurements** indicate informed methodological choices.  However, the procedure relies on indirect determination of braking force (via F = m g sinÎ¸ â F_d) rather than direct measurement, and the rationale for choosing Fââv as the plotted dependent variable reveals some conceptual compromise.  Some potential sources of systematic error (e.g., skin effects, field non-uniformity) are recognised only after data collection.  Overall the methodology is mostly complete and appropriate, hence top of the 3-4 band (score 4) rather than fully comprehensive.

The essay demonstrates **solid command of electromagnetism**: Faradayâs law, Lorentz force, Lenzâs law, skin effect and finite-element references are all accurately employed to construct a macroscopic model, culminating in Fâ â BÂ².  Explanations are generally correct and supported by appropriate literature, and the student applies concepts (e.g., vector cross product, resistance influence) to justify design decisions.  Occasional simplifications (perfectly perpendicular field lines, uniform flux) introduce approximations, but do not significantly undermine the depth of understanding.  Performance aligns with the 5-6 band; minor inaccuracies preclude the maximum, so a score of 5 is assigned.

The student reflects on **assumptions, systematic errors (skin effect, field angle), and equipment limitations**, and proposes sensible improvements (rotating brake rig, constant-speed motor, direct force measurement).  The evaluation recognises sources of uncertainty and their likely impact on results, but often relies on qualitative assertions rather than quantitative re-analysis.  Some evaluative comments appear after conclusions instead of being integrated within the argument, and there is limited exploration of alternative methodologies or theoretical challenges.  Overall this represents a **good level of critical evaluation**, consistent with the upper end of the 4-6 band; score 6 is justified.

The student presents a **precise, engaging title and immediately states the research topic and its practical context** (eddy-current braking in roller coasters). Throughout the essay the focus on âmagnetic-flux density versus braking force for a track of fixed geometry and friction coefficientâ is sustained; sectional headings, diagrams and repeated signposting keep the reader oriented.  Explanations are coherent and sufficiently detailed for a non-specialist examiner to follow the argument, indicating **clear purpose and effective communication**. Only minor lapses in concision (occasional repetition) prevent a flawless performance, but these do not obscure the topic. Hence the top band is appropriate and the higher mark (6) is warranted.

Visual presentation is generally **effective**: diagrams, photographs and graphs enhance understanding, colours are used judiciously, and captions explain content.  Consistent font and spacing are maintained, and tables are neatly formatted.  However, a few images contain lengthy alt-text-style captions within the main body, and one placeholder graphic appears incomplete, slightly disrupting flow.  These minor inconsistencies place the work solidly within the top band but closer to its lower end; a score of 3 is appropriate.

The research questionââHow does the magnetic flux density impact the magnitude of the braking force due to induced eddy currents for a track of constant dimensions and coefficient of static friction?ââis **explicit, focused and directly guides the investigation**.  Control of ancillary factors (track dimensions, separation, angle, resistance) is stated, showing strong alignment between question and ensuing discussion.  Minor slippage occurs when the measured quantity becomes Fââv rather than Fâ itself, indicating a **small loss of focus**; nevertheless, the link between question, theory and data remains strong.  This merits the upper band, but with a score of 5 rather than 6.

The student undertakes **detailed quantitative analysis**: multiple trials, propagation of uncertainties, linear regressions, RÂ² evaluation and comparison with literature models.  Data are transformed (Fââv vs BÂ²) to test the theoretical relationship, and sources of error are considered in estimating uncertainties.  The analysis remains clearly linked to the research question, and conclusions are drawn directly from processed data.  Minor weaknesses include dependence on derived rather than measured force and limited statistical treatment (e.g., no confidence intervals on gradient), which temper sophistication.  These features place the work solidly in the 7-9 band; score 8 reflects strong but not flawless analytical quality.

The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page with word count, abstract omitted as per current guide, detailed table of contents, numbered pages, clearly delineated sections, accurate in-text citations and bibliography.  Figures, tables and appendices are labelled and cross-referenced.  Minor issues (occasional long captions, slight over-length figure descriptions) do not impede navigation or comprehension.  Hence the work meets all expectations for a clear and appropriate structure, meriting the top band (3-4) with a score of 4.

annotations.comment: The evaluation describes systematic errors qualitatively but lacks quantitative error estimates; consider estimating the magnitude of perpendicularity deviations and skin-effect corrections to deepen critical evaluation.

The title and research question are communicated precisely, establishing a clear investigative focus on how magnetic flux density affects eddy-current braking force. This immediately orients the reader to the core purpose and scope of the study, demonstrating excellent clarity and effective topic communication.
Including the word count on the title page meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative guidelines, ensuring compliance with report conventions.
The layout of the title page is professional and well-balanced, with clear hierarchy between title, research question, and administrative details, supporting effective visual communication.
The Table of Contents is comprehensive and formatted consistently, with clear section headings and page references that facilitate easy navigation of the essay structure.

The anecdotal introduction provides engagement but could be condensed to focus more directly on the scientific rationale rather than personal observations, sharpening alignment with the research scope.
The research question is stated clearly in bold, ensuring that the investigationâs aim is explicit and strongly connected to the subsequent methodological and theoretical discussions.

The background section accurately describes electromagnetic induction qualitatively but does not yet integrate quantitative expressions; consider introducing key equations earlier to strengthen subject-knowledge application.
The free-body diagram clearly identifies all forces on the inclined cart, supporting the subsequent mathematical treatment of equilibrium and aiding conceptual understanding of the force balance.
Using the static friction coefficient Î¼_s at terminal velocity may be inappropriate since dynamic friction applies during motion; clarify the distinction and justify the choice of Î¼_s versus Î¼_k.
Fig. 1 caption is verbose and embedded in alt text; streamline the caption to a concise description and relocate detailed alt text to the image description to improve readability.

The assumption cos Î¸ = 1 simplifies the derivation but warrants quantitative discussion of the small-angle approximationâs validity; include an estimate of Î¸ deviation to bolster critical evaluation.
Adapting the patent design to moving magnets is practical, but the justification for this choice should be expanded to explain how it preserves experimental validity relative to fixed-magnet setups.
Photographs of the apparatus lack a scale bar or ruler reference for dimensional context; adding scale annotations would enhance reproducibility and interpretability of the experimental setup.

The increments in magnetic flux density are unevenly spaced (e.g., 43â103â141 mT); provide a rationale for these choices to demonstrate deliberate experimental design rather than arbitrary selection.
The Works Cited title is formatted consistently with section headers, demonstrating clear adherence to formatting conventions and effective visual organization.
The inverse-cube law is cited from Griffiths but without a page reference; include precise citations for textbook sources when invoking specific laws to reinforce academic rigor.

The discussion of MaxwellâAmpÃ¨reâs law conflates electric and magnetic field induction; clarify the distinct roles of Faradayâs and AmpÃ¨reâs laws and their synthesis to avoid conceptual confusion.

When introducing F_m = BIL, the variable L is defined ambiguously; provide a clear definition of L early (e.g., mean eddy current path length) to ensure precise application of the macroscopic Lorentz model.

Fig. 2 on defect impacts is interesting but not directly related to magnetic braking on flat conductors; consider removing or better integrating this example to maintain focus on the research question.
Calculating braking force by dividing by mass addresses variable cart mass post-trial, but controlling mass directly (e.g., adding ballast) could more robustly isolate the flux-force relationship.
The list of control variables is comprehensive, but the formatting of sub-lists and subscripts (e.g., Î¼_d vs Î¼_s) is inconsistent; standardize list styles and notation for clarity.

The qualitative observation of heating is noted, but no temperature measurements or uncertainty estimates are provided; consider quantifying the thermal effect to strengthen evaluation of energy dissipation.
Graph 1âs multiple colored trend lines overlap and lack distinct markers; use varied line styles or annotate specific slopes directly to improve readability and differentiation.
Graph 2 labels terminal velocity in mm sâ»Â¹ while theoretical derivations use m sâ»Â¹; ensure consistency of units across analysis and visualizations to prevent unit-conversion errors.
Graph 3 demonstrates an excellent linear fit of Fâ/v vs. BÂ² with RÂ² = 0.9908, providing strong empirical support for the theoretical proportionality and reflecting rigorous data analysis.

Introducing external Graph 4 on Fâ â v is valuable but interrupts the flow; integrate this literature comparison earlier or discuss its relevance immediately to maintain coherence of your argument.
The rotating-drum apparatus proposal is insightful but would benefit from a schematic outline or component list to make the future methodology suggestion more concrete and actionable.

The Works Cited list mixes citation styles and lacks uniform formatting (e.g., inconsistent punctuation, missing journal issue numbers); standardize to a single style guide to enhance presentation quality.

kept controlled?
